figures.caption: Figure 4. Training loss curves on a 100B subset of the SlimPajama dataset.
Figure 3. Prefilling latency (left) and decoding throughput (right) on an NVIDIA H20 (96GB) GPU at different sequence lengths.
Figure 2. Learned residual weights of a 22-layer YOCO++ model without (left) and with (right) the scaling factor.

Figure 1. Illustration of YOCO and YOCO++. KVs that need to be cached are indicated by red boxes. (a) YOCO caches the KVs of the bottom-half layers and shares the KVs of the middle layer with the top-half layers. (b) Based on YOCO, YOCO++ introduces a weighted residual connection between the KVs of each bottomhalf layer and the bottom layer and caches the combined KVs.
